prompt.content:
You are a spatial reasoning assistant analyzing a 3D scene image.
You will receive a list of objects visible in the image and a set of distance comparison questions.

For each question, compare 3D distances — either between two pairs of objects,
or from a common anchor object to two candidate objects.
Answer with exactly one of: "<" (first pair/candidate closer), "~=" (approximately equal), ">" (first pair/candidate farther).

Respond ONLY with a JSON array. Each element must have "qid" and "answer" (a string: "<", "~=", or ">").

Example:
[{"qid": "qrr_0001", "answer": "<"}, {"qid": "qrr_0002", "answer": "~="}]

content.text:
Objects in the image:
  - obj_0: small purple metal cylinder
  - obj_1: small brown rubber cube
  - obj_2: large green rubber cylinder
  - obj_3: small yellow metal cylinder
  - obj_4: large blue metal cube
  - obj_5: large gray rubber cube
  - obj_6: small green metal cube
  - obj_7: small cyan rubber cylinder
  - obj_8: small purple rubber cylinder
  - obj_9: small brown rubber cube

Questions:
[qrr_0261] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_7. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0262] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_8. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0263] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_4 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0264] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_5 and obj_6. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0265] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_5 and obj_7. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0266] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_5 and obj_8. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0267] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_5 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0268] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_6 and obj_7. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0269] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_6 and obj_8. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0270] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_6 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0271] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_7 and obj_8. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0272] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_7 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0273] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_3. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0274] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_5. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0275] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_6. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0276] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_7. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0277] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_8. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0278] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0279] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_5. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0280] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_6. Answer: < / ~= / >

raw_response:
I need to analyze the 3D scene and compare distances between pairs of objects. Let me first estimate the positions of each object based on the image.

From the image, I'll estimate approximate 3D positions (x, y, z):
- obj_0 (small purple metal cylinder): right side, front area (~6, 0, 2)
- obj_1 (small brown rubber cube): left-center, back (~1, 0, -1)
- obj_2 (large green rubber cylinder): front-center (~2, 0, 3)
- obj_3 (small yellow metal cylinder): back-center (~3, 0, -3)
- obj_4 (large blue metal cube): center (~3, 0, -1)
- obj_5 (large gray rubber cube): right (~7, 0, 1)
- obj_6 (small green metal cube): far left (~-1, 0, 1)
- obj_7 (small cyan rubber cylinder): front-left (~1, 0, 2)
- obj_8 (small purple rubber cylinder): back-right (~4, 0, -2)
- obj_9 (small brown rubber cube): right-center (~5, 0, -1)

Let me re-examine the image more carefully to get better position estimates.

Looking at the image:
- obj_6 (green metal cube): far left
- obj_1 (brown rubber cube): left-center area, back
- obj_7 (cyan cylinder): front-left
- obj_2 (green cylinder): front-center
- obj_3 (yellow cylinder): back, behind blue cube
- obj_4 (blue metal cube): center-right, behind
- obj_8 (purple rubber cylinder): back-right area
- obj_9 (brown rubber cube): right of center
- obj_0 (purple metal cylinder): right side, front
- obj_5 (gray cube): far right

Estimated coordinates (x, z) roughly:
- obj_1: (2, -1)
- obj_2: (3, 4)
- obj_3: (4, -4)
- obj_4: (5, -2)
- obj_5: (8, 0)
- obj_6: (0, 0)
- obj_7: (2, 2)
- obj_8: (5, -3)
- obj_9: (6, -1)

Now computing distances:

dist(obj_1, obj_3) = sqrt((2-4)²+(-1-(-4))²) = sqrt(4+9) = sqrt(13) ≈ 3.6

qrr_0261: dist(obj_1,obj_3) vs dist(obj_4,obj_7): dist(obj_4,obj_7)=sqrt((5-2)²+(-2-2)²)=sqrt(9+16)=5. 3.6 < 5 → <
qrr_0262: dist(obj_4,obj_8)=sqrt((5-5)²+(-2-(-3))²)=sqrt(0+1)=1. 3.6 > 1 → >
qrr_0263: dist(obj_4,obj_9)=sqrt((5-6)²+(-2-(-1))²)=sqrt(1+1)≈1.4. 3.6 > 1.4 → >
qrr_0264: dist(obj_5,obj_6)=sqrt((8-0)²+(0-0)²)=8. 3.6 < 8 → <
qrr_0265: dist(obj_5,obj_7)=sqrt((8-2)²+(0-2)²)=sqrt(36+4)≈6.3. 3.6 < 6.3 → <
qrr_0266: dist(obj_5,obj_8)=sqrt((8-5)²+(0-(-3))²)=sqrt(9+9)≈4.2. 3.6 < 4.2 → <
qrr_0267: dist(obj_5,obj_9)=sqrt((8-6)²+(0-(-1))²)=sqrt(4+1)≈2.2. 3.6 > 2.2 → >
qrr_0268: dist(obj_6,obj_7)=sqrt((0-2)²+(0-2)²)=sqrt(8)≈2.8. 3.6 > 2.8 → >
qrr_0269: dist(obj_6,obj_8)=sqrt((0-5)²+(0-(-3))²)=sqrt(25+9)≈5.8. 3.6 < 5.8 → <
qrr_0270: dist(obj_6,obj_9)=sqrt((0-6)²+(0-(-1))²)=sqrt(36+1)≈6.1. 3.6 < 6.1 → <
qrr_0271: dist(obj_7,obj_8)=sqrt((2-5)²+(2-(-3))²)=sqrt(9+25)≈5.8. 3.6 < 5.8 → <
qrr_0272: dist(obj_7,obj_9)=sqrt((2-6)²+(2-(-1))²)=sqrt(16+9)=5. 3.6 < 5 → <

dist(obj_1,obj_4)=sqrt((2-5)²+(-1-(-2))²)=sqrt(9+1)≈3.2

qrr_0273: dist(obj_2,obj_3)=sqrt((3-4)²+(4-(-4))²)=sqrt(1+64)≈8.1. 3.2 < 8.1 → <
qrr_0274: dist(obj_2,obj_5)=sqrt((3-8)²+(4-0)²)=sqrt(25+16)≈6.4. 3.2 < 6.4 → <
qrr_0275: dist(obj_2,obj_6)=sqrt((3-0)²+(4-0)²)=sqrt(9+16)=5. 3.2 < 5 → <
qrr_0276: dist(obj_2,obj_7)=sqrt((3-2)²+(4-2)²)=sqrt(1+4)≈2.2. 3.2 > 2.2 → >
qrr_0277: dist(obj_2,obj_8)=sqrt((3-5)²+(4-(-3))²)=sqrt(4+49)≈7.3. 3.2 < 7.3 → <
qrr_0278: dist(obj_2,obj_9)=sqrt((3-6)²+(4-(-1))²)=sqrt(9+25)≈5.8. 3.2 < 5.8 → <
qrr_0279: dist(obj_3,obj_5)=sqrt((4-8)²+(-4-0)²)=sqrt(16+16)≈5.7. 3.2 < 5.7 → <
qrr_0280: dist(obj_3,obj_6)=sqrt((4-0)²+(-4-0)²)=sqrt(16+16)≈5.7. 3.2 < 5.7 → <
